financial.note: A major Taiwanese creditor and shareholder (~15.8%); interest rate hikes in Taiwan or Thailand increase the cost of short-term OD and long-term borrowing.
Acts as a primary financial intermediary and major shareholder; provides liquidity and credit facilities for regional expansion.

supply_chain.note: CCET is a critical PCBA and component manufacturer for the HDD cluster in Thailand; global storage demand directly drives volume.
CCET is a critical PCBA and component manufacturer for the HDD cluster in Thailand; global storage demand directly drives volume.
Major manufacturer of ink-jet and laser printers; global consumer spending on home office tech impacts quarterly revenues.

Raw material costs; global semiconductor supply constraints or price hikes for chipsets used in AI/PCBA applications compress margins.

competitors.note: Competition for high-value power electronics and EV component manufacturing capacity.
Direct rival in EMS (Electronic Manufacturing Services) and semiconductor packaging.
Competes in industrial and telecom hardware manufacturing segments.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: As a 100% exporter, a weaker THB inflates THB-denominated revenue, though it increases the cost of imported raw materials.
Correlates with global demand for electronics; high index readings signal a robust order book for EMS providers.
High logistics costs for global exports (especially to US/EU) directly impact the net profit margins of the company.
